mechanism_narrative: A rising level of nonfarm payroll employment indicates expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The level of nonfarm payroll employment produces a rank correlation below 0.03 with twenty-day S&P 500 forward returns over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above seven percent signals substantial labor market slack that sustains investor expectations of accommodative monetary policy, compressing the equity risk premium and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: When the unemployment rate exceeds seven percent, S&P 500 five-day forward returns are negative in more than fifty percent of triggered instances over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the level of retail sales and the level of industrial production captures the joint strength of consumption and manufacturing activity; this interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between retail sales and industrial production yields a rank correlation below 0.03 with sixty-day forward returns over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below twenty-five basis points suppresses benchmark Treasury yields and forces fixed-income investors to reallocate into equities, producing a persistent positive risk premium in S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days until policy lift-off begins.
failure_condition: During periods when the federal funds rate is below twenty-five basis points, the average twenty-day S&P 500 return is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p-value greater than 0.05) over any two-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below ninety-nine reflects contracting manufacturing utilization and weaker capacity demand, which triggers downward earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production below ninety-nine coincides with positive twenty-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty-five percent of instances over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding eight hundred thousand units signal durable construction demand and upstream commodity strength, which feeds into broad cyclical earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts above eight hundred thousand units are followed by negative sixty-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty percent of occurrences during any three-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the consumer price index and the federal funds rate captures the real monetary policy stance; elevated CPIAUCSL combined with suppressed FEDFUNDS forces capital into risk assets and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of the consumer price index and the federal funds rate produces a rank information coefficient below 0.03 for five-day forward returns over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP exceeding thirteen thousand five hundred billion chained 2017 dollars indicates the economy has recovered past recession trough levels, anchoring positive long-term earnings growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Real GDP above thirteen thousand five hundred billion coincides with negative sixty-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty percent of observations over any three-year window.
